records.ety: [Prob. from OF. brossequin, or D. broosken. See Brodekin.]

defs: A strong, protecting covering for the foot, coming some distance up the leg.
quotes: The hunted red deer's undressed hide Their hairy buskins well supplied.

defs: A similar covering for the foot and leg, made with very thick soles, to give an appearance of elevation to the stature; -- worn by tragic actors in ancient Greece and Rome. Used as a symbol of tragedy, or the tragic drama, as distinguished from comedy.
quotes: Great Fletcher never treads in buskins here, No greater Jonson dares in socks appear.
